steps.message:
[SYSTEM]
You are a careful and methodical reasoner.
Each turn, you MUST respond with ONLY a JSON block in this exact format:
```json
{
  "analysis": "<your observations and reasoning this turn>",
  "plan": "<what you intend to examine or verify next>",
  "answers": null
}
```
When you have verified all answers and are fully confident, set `answers` to a list of strings (one letter per question, in question order), for example:
```json
{
  "analysis": "<final verification notes>",
  "plan": "done",
  "answers": ["B", "A", "C"]
}
```
Do NOT set `answers` until you have genuinely cross-checked every question. Take as many turns as you need.

[USER]
You are solving a set of orthographic projection questions.

Each question is presented as a single composite image in standard PAT format:
- Left panel:  top view, front view, and end view, separated by orthographic reference lines. One of them has been replaced by a question mark. 
- Right panel: four candidate views for the question mark labelled A, B, C, D,

Select the one view that is fully consistent with the two other views.

Respond each turn with a JSON block as instructed. When you are confident in all your answers, set the "answers" field to a list of letters (one per question, in order).

There are 15 question(s) in total. The composite question image(s) follow.

{
  "analysis": "Initial pass completed on all 15. Best-fit choices from orthographic consistency:
q01: circular top with centered hidden square and bottom tab; no side tab and no triangle -> D.
q02: front view must show symmetric hull/trapezoid from top, central vertical post, hidden circular feature; option B matches overall silhouette and hidden lines best.
q03: top has left strip and right diagonal depth feature; front has stepped height with hidden vertical+horizontal corner. End view should show rectangle with upper-right visible recess and hidden left vertical -> A.
q04: object has rounded/elliptic side profile in top implied by front/end; top must show ellipse with rectangular right projection and lower tab hidden/visible arrangement -> B.
q05: end view of tapered square frustum on base with central stem/crossbar; should show base rectangle left, tapered visible side right with hidden internal slant/stem -> A.
q06: top view from front/end gives outer plan with bottom central notch, top-left notch, right-side extension visible, internal rectangular feature, and no circular hole -> D.
q07: top is rectangle with circular hole; end shows triangular profile plus vertical post, so front collapses to rectangle with two hidden verticals for cylindrical hole -> D.
q08: from top/front, end should be stepped rectangular profile, not slanted exterior, with hidden pair near right from central slot -> B.
q09: elongated end profile with top boss near right; hidden detail on left and underside steps best align with B.
q10: top of frustum with through horizontal bar and central top boss; top outline should be square/frustum ring, not plain square only; hidden line through bar from internal opening -> D.
q11: circular plan with large top-right recess, small bottom-right notch, left circular hole visible, bottom tab visible, and two hidden rectangular features from projections -> C.
q12: circular outer plan with bottom opening, top circle overlapping left rectangle, and central lower rectangular tongue. Hidden arc orientation from front/end best matches B.
q13: end view should show rectangular base with centered spike, hidden verticals, and visible small rectangular notch near center-bottom; no side protrusion in end -> C.
q14: front view from top/end must include left lower protrusion, top central twin post, hidden verticals, and visible diagonal on lower center-right -> B.
q15: top view should be circular outer plan with inner circular opening, central visible vertical stem, hidden centerlines, and small right rectangular feature visible -> B.
Need only brief final cross-check: uncertain items were q02, q04, q10, q12; after rechecking relative visible/hidden edges and projection placement, chosen set remains most consistent.",
  "plan": "done",
  "answers": ["D", "B", "A", "B", "A", "D", "D", "B", "B", "D", "C", "B", "C", "B", "B"]
}
